response:
The rental market in Miami, Florida, shows a wide range of prices. Single family homes have the highest mean prices. Condos and apartments show lower median values. Standard deviation indicates high price variability. Outliers are common in luxury properties. Filtering these helps understand market trends.

* * *

Visualizing the data with Seaborn reveals clear price differences. Each property type has its own distribution curve. Condos cluster around the median price. Single family homes spread out more. Outliers appear as distant points on the plot. This highlights market extremes.

* * *

Segmenting by property type allows deeper insights. Luxury condos have higher standard deviations. Apartments show more consistent pricing. The analysis reveals market diversity. Visualization supports data-driven decisions. Miami's rental market is dynamic and varied.
